commands.author: adapepper
commands.command: — addressing the codex review for `70700cf707` (now `cb30b0d382`). ## p2: encode non-ascii filenames in content-disposition — fixed > "node's `serverresponse.setheader` throws `err_invalid_char` for names like > `価格.xlsx`, and because this call is inside the route `try`, those downloads > become a 404. use an ascii fallback plus rfc 5987 `filename*`, and cover it > with a real header-validation regression." done in `cb30b0d382`. new helper `builddownloadcontentdispositionheader()` emits rfc 5987 / 6266 §3.2.4-compliant headers: - **pure ascii** (e.g. `pricing.xlsx`) → `attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx"` (legacy single-token form preserved; no churn on the common case) - **non-ascii** (e.g. `価格.xlsx`, `résumé.docx`) → `attachment; filename="<ascii-fallback>"; filename*=utf-8''<percent-encoded>` (latin-1-safe fallback for legacy clients, utf-8-precise `filename*` for modern browsers and curl) ```diff +function builddownloadcontentdispositionheader(safefilename: string): string { + const islatin1safe = /^[\x20-\x7e\xa0-\xff]*$/u.test(safefilename); + const asciifallback = safefilename.replace(/[^\x20-\x7e]/gu, "_"); + const utf8encoded = encodeuricomponent(safefilename) + .replace(/['()]/g, (c) => "%" + c.charcodeat(0).tostring(16).touppercase()) + .replace(/\*/g, "%2a"); + if (islatin1safe && asciifallback === safefilename) { + return `attachment; filename="${safefilename}"`; + } + return `attachment; filename="${asciifallback}"; filename*=utf-8''${utf8encoded}`; +} ``` ### real header-validation regression coverage — added three new tests in `control-ui.http.test.ts` use a real `http.createserver()` (not `makemockhttpresponse`, which codex correctly noted does not enforce node's runtime header character validation): ``` ✓ serves a cjk filename without err_invalid_char (uses rfc 5987 filename*) ✓ serves a latin-1 accented filename via the ascii fallback path ✓ keeps the legacy single-token form for pure-ascii filenames ``` `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts`: **165/165 passing** (was 156; +9 = 3 tests × 3 vitest projects). ## p3: changelog entry — added ``` ### fixes - gateway/chat: serve assistant-media binary downloads (xlsx/csv/pdf/zip/etc) with `content-disposition: attachment` and an rfc 5987 / 6266 `filename*=utf-8''…` value plus an ascii fallback so non-image documents trigger a real download instead of letting the browser render them as a webpage and offer save as "webpage complete". document attachment anchors in the chat surface also receive a `download` attribute and the managed `/api/chat/media/outgoing-doc/` url is no longer double-wrapped through `/__openclaw__/assistant-media`. (#77912) ``` ## real behavior proof — before/after with a real http.server drives the patched handler against three real fixtures via `http.createserver()` + `http.request()` to 127.0.0.1. ### after fix (`cb30b0d382`) ``` === pricing.xlsx (pure ascii — control) === http 200 content-type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet content-disposition: attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx" ✓ pass === résumé.docx (latin-1 accented) === http 200 content-type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document content-disposition: attachment; filename="r_sum_.docx"; filename*=utf-8''r%c3%a9sum%c3%a9.docx ✓ pass === 価格.xlsx (cjk — was throwing err_invalid_char before this fix) === http 200 content-type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet content-disposition: attachment; filename="__.xlsx"; filename*=utf-8''%e4%be%a1%e6%a0%bc.xlsx ✓ pass 3 passed, 0 failed ``` ### before fix (same harness, fix reverted via `git stash`) ``` === pricing.xlsx (pure ascii — control) === http 200 content-disposition: attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx" ✓ pass === résumé.docx (latin-1 accented) === http 200 content-disposition: attachment; filename="résumé.docx" ✗ fail (works on node since é fits in latin-1, but no `filename*` for non-rfc-5987 clients) === 価格.xlsx (cjk — was throwing err_invalid_char before this fix) === http 404 ← ← ← codex's exact prediction content-type: text/plain; charset=utf-8 content-disposition: undefined ✗ fail 1 passed, 2 failed ``` that **http 404 for `価格.xlsx`** is the user-visible breakage: node's `setheader("content-disposition", \`attachment; filename="価格.xlsx"\`)` throws `err_invalid_char` inside the route try/catch, the exception is swallowed, and the download dies. exactly what codex described. ## acceptance criteria | criterion | status | |---|---| | `pnpm test src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` | ✓ 165/165 (was 156) | | `pnpm test src/gateway/control-ui-assistant-media.e2e.test.ts` | (separate suite, untouched) | | `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/control-ui.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts changelog.md` | ✓ clean | artifacts: `~/.openclaw/workspace/data/openclaw-pr-proofs/77912/`. cc @vincentkoc @steipete — please re-review.
commands.freeform_prompt: — addressing the Codex review for `70700cf707` (now `cb30b0d382`). ## P2: Encode non-ASCII filenames in Content-Disposition — FIXED > "Node's `ServerResponse.setHeader` throws `ERR_INVALID_CHAR` for names like > `価格.xlsx`, and because this call is inside the route `try`, those downloads > become a 404. Use an ASCII fallback plus RFC 5987 `filename*`, and cover it > with a real header-validation regression." Done in `cb30b0d382`. New helper `buildDownloadContentDispositionHeader()` emits RFC 5987 / 6266 §3.2.4-compliant headers: - **Pure ASCII** (e.g. `pricing.xlsx`) → `attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx"` (legacy single-token form preserved; no churn on the common case) - **Non-ASCII** (e.g. `価格.xlsx`, `résumé.docx`) → `attachment; filename="<ascii-fallback>"; filename*=UTF-8''<percent-encoded>` (Latin-1-safe fallback for legacy clients, UTF-8-precise `filename*` for modern browsers and curl) ```diff +function buildDownloadContentDispositionHeader(safeFilename: string): string { + const isLatin1Safe = /^[\x20-\x7E\xA0-\xFF]*$/u.test(safeFilename); + const asciiFallback = safeFilename.replace(/[^\x20-\x7E]/gu, "_"); + const utf8Encoded = encodeURIComponent(safeFilename) + .replace(/['()]/g, (c) => "%" + c.charCodeAt(0).toString(16).toUpperCase()) + .replace(/\*/g, "%2A"); + if (isLatin1Safe && asciiFallback === safeFilename) { + return `attachment; filename="${safeFilename}"`; + } + return `attachment; filename="${asciiFallback}"; filename*=UTF-8''${utf8Encoded}`; +} ``` ### Real header-validation regression coverage — ADDED Three new tests in `control-ui.http.test.ts` use a real `http.createServer()` (not `makeMockHttpResponse`, which Codex correctly noted does not enforce Node's runtime header character validation): ``` ✓ serves a CJK filename without ERR_INVALID_CHAR (uses RFC 5987 filename*) ✓ serves a Latin-1 accented filename via the ASCII fallback path ✓ keeps the legacy single-token form for pure-ASCII filenames ``` `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts`: **165/165 passing** (was 156; +9 = 3 tests × 3 vitest projects). ## P3: CHANGELOG entry — ADDED ``` ### Fixes - Gateway/chat: serve assistant-media binary downloads (xlsx/csv/pdf/zip/etc) with `Content-Disposition: attachment` and an RFC 5987 / 6266 `filename*=UTF-8''…` value plus an ASCII fallback so non-image documents trigger a real download instead of letting the browser render them as a webpage and offer Save As "Webpage Complete". Document attachment anchors in the chat surface also receive a `download` attribute and the managed `/api/chat/media/outgoing-doc/` URL is no longer double-wrapped through `/__openclaw__/assistant-media`. (#77912) ``` ## Real behavior proof — before/after with a real http.Server Drives the patched handler against three real fixtures via `http.createServer()` + `http.request()` to 127.0.0.1. ### After fix (`cb30b0d382`) ``` === pricing.xlsx (pure ASCII — control) === HTTP 200 content-type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet content-disposition: attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx" ✓ PASS === résumé.docx (Latin-1 accented) === HTTP 200 content-type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document content-disposition: attachment; filename="r_sum_.docx"; filename*=UTF-8''r%C3%A9sum%C3%A9.docx ✓ PASS === 価格.xlsx (CJK — was throwing ERR_INVALID_CHAR before this fix) === HTTP 200 content-type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet content-disposition: attachment; filename="__.xlsx"; filename*=UTF-8''%E4%BE%A1%E6%A0%BC.xlsx ✓ PASS 3 passed, 0 failed ``` ### Before fix (same harness, fix reverted via `git stash`) ``` === pricing.xlsx (pure ASCII — control) === HTTP 200 content-disposition: attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx" ✓ PASS === résumé.docx (Latin-1 accented) === HTTP 200 content-disposition: attachment; filename="résumé.docx" ✗ FAIL (works on Node since é fits in Latin-1, but no `filename*` for non-RFC-5987 clients) === 価格.xlsx (CJK — was throwing ERR_INVALID_CHAR before this fix) === HTTP 404 ← ← ← Codex's exact prediction content-type: text/plain; charset=utf-8 content-disposition: undefined ✗ FAIL 1 passed, 2 failed ``` That **HTTP 404 for `価格.xlsx`** is the user-visible breakage: Node's `setHeader("Content-Disposition", \`attachment; filename="価格.xlsx"\`)` throws `ERR_INVALID_CHAR` inside the route try/catch, the exception is swallowed, and the download dies. Exactly what Codex described. ## Acceptance criteria | Criterion | Status | |---|---| | `pnpm test src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` | ✓ 165/165 (was 156) | | `pnpm test src/gateway/control-ui-assistant-media.e2e.test.ts` | (separate suite, untouched) | | `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/control-ui.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts CHANGELOG.md` | ✓ clean | Artifacts: `~/.openclaw/workspace/data/openclaw-pr-proofs/77912/`. cc @vincentkoc @steipete — please re-review.
